title: allium cyprium subsp. lefkarense
intro: Allium cyprium subsp. lefkarense, also known as Lefkara garlic, is a species of plant belonging to the Amaryllidaceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including antibacterial, antiviral, and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of consuming Lefkara garlic include reducing the risk of heart disease and certain types of cancer, as well as improving respiratory health. In herbal preparations, Lefkara garlic is often used in the form of infusions, capsules, or as a topical application, but its exact usage may vary depending on the region and cultural practices.
